truthAudit.Summary: Audit of Run 2 against ground truth reveals 6 true vulnerabilities correctly identified, 14 missed, and 1 unsupported finding. Several severity ratings in the previous answer were misaligned with the ground truth and require correction.

Items.Notes: The previous answer correctly identified the format string vulnerability in the logging function and provided the exact code snippet.
The integer overflow in the factorial computation was not mentioned in the previous answer.
Use-after-free in memory pool cleanup via deferred history flush was not identified.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: std::string command = validator_cmd + " " + config_file; int result = system(command.c_str());
Items.Notes: The previous answer correctly identified command injection in config validation with the exact code quote.
The previous answer correctly identified the stack buffer overflow in input processing.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: volatile long long value_; ... long long get() const { return value_; }
Items.Notes: The previous answer correctly identified the race condition in the thread-safe counter.
The logic bomb in authentication via emergency override was not mentioned.
Path traversal in configuration loading was not identified.
The previous answer correctly identified the heap overflow in the expression parser.
The previous answer correctly identified the hardcoded administrator credentials.
Weak PRNG for session tokens was not mentioned.
Thread-unsafe timestamp cache in logging was not identified.
Insecure temporary file handling was not mentioned.
Persistent authentication state was not identified.
Unhandled exception in variable parsing was not mentioned.
Integer underflow in memory-pool block split was not identified.
Unbounded recursion in parser was not mentioned.
Data race in result cache was not identified.
TOCTOU in config loading was not mentioned.
Integer division by zero in command routing was not identified.

Items.Key: os command injection in admin console|command injection|cwe-78|critical|0.98|enhanced_calc.cpp|605|609|admin::adminconsole::execute_admin_command|if (command.substr(0, 4) == "exec") { std::string cmd = command.substr(5); system(cmd.c_str()); }|authenticated attackers can execute arbitrary os commands with the privileges of the running process.|authenticated admin sends input starting with 'exec ' followed by a shell command.|remove system() calls; implement a strict whitelist of allowed commands or use safe execution apis with argument parsing.->os command injection in admin console|command injection|cwe-78|critical|0.98|enhanced_calc.cpp|605|609|admin::adminconsole::execute_admin_command|if (command.substr(0, 4) == "exec") { std::string cmd = command.substr(5); system(cmd.c_str()); }|authenticated attackers can execute arbitrary os commands with the privileges of the running process.|authenticated admin sends input starting with 'exec ' followed by a shell command.|remove system() calls; implement a strict whitelist of allowed commands or use safe execution apis with argument parsing.
